page: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-8 is a general-purpose server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs, operating with a 1.0 thread-per-core topology on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPU hardware, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance scoring in the top 25% at 2688.09. Multi-core performance is average, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 21511.57 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 7817.0. Memory bandwidth is stable, reaching 996.35 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 95.12 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. The instance also demonstrates strong text generation speeds for small language models, achieving 256.59 tokens per second on a 135M parameter model, while medium 7B model inference runs at average speeds.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the t2d-standard-8 offers a balanced resource density for general-purpose workloads, utilizing dedicated physical cores to avoid resource contention without the premium cost of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated tiers. The lack of local storage requires external block storage attachment, which is a key architectural tradeoff. This server is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, including static web serving, software compilation, database operations, and small-scale language model inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-8 is a general-purpose server configured with 8 vCPUs and 8 dedicated physical AMD EPYC 7B13 cores on the x86_64 architecture. It features 32.0 GB of RAM, yielding 4.0 GB of memory per core, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU performance and strong text generation speeds for small language models, alongside average multi-core and memory bandwidth results. This instance provides a balanced resource profile, making it a cost-effective option for workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or massive memory arrays. It is suited for static web serving, database operations, software compilation, and light machine learning inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-8 is a general-purpose server with 8 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 32 GB RAM, and strong single-core performance, suited for web serving and database workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-8 is an 8-vCPU, 32 GB RAM general-purpose server powered by dedicated AMD EPYC 7B13 processors.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC server offering balanced compute and memory resources for general-purpose workloads and web serving.

32.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core
Strong single-core CPU performance and small-model text generation speeds
